entry.title: The Faces of Energy and Natural Resources at SAP
entry.content:
Energy and natural resources keep the world going.  The Energy and Natural Resources team at SAP is dedicated to fostering innovation and excellence across their industries. This team includes experts from the oil, gas & energy, utilities, chemicals, mill products, and mining industries.

By actively engaging with customers, partners, and stakeholders, and anticipating future trends, the team formulates strategies and solutions. Their collaborative efforts ensure they meet industry needs, stay at the forefront of advancements, drive positive change, and create value for our customers.

Recently, Torsten Welte took over the position as head of Energy and Natural Resources. Check out his announcement here: 

 

In the ongoing series Faces of ENR, Torsten talks to different team members every week, discovering their personal motivation and connection to the industry, current topics, and the value SAP delivers for each industry.

Mill Products and Mining

 

Utilities

 

 

 

 

Chemicals

Stay tuned for the first Chemicals video, appearing soon.

Oil, Gas, and Energy

Torsten's conversations with our oil, gas, and energy colleagues will be published soon. 

Stay connected to meet further team members and to learn more about the passionate team driving innovation for Utilities, Oil, Gas & Energy, Mill & Mining, and Chemicals.

entry.title: IBP FINAL ROP
entry.content:
Hi,

I have this below key figure calculation

ZFINALROP@REQUEST = SUM("ZFINALROP@ZWKPRODLOCNOPER")

ZFINALROP@WKPRODLOC = ROUND(IF(ISNULL("ZADJUSTEDROP@WKPRODLOC"),"ROP@WKPRODLOC","ZADJUSTEDROP@WKPRODLOC"),0)

ZFINALROP@ZWKPRODLOCNOPER = IBP_LPA("ZFINALROP@WKPRODLOC")

ZFINALROP@CALWKPRODLOC = SUM("ZFINALROP@WKPRODLOC")

 

I just want to have tech week A value copied to B always what should I make a change above to not affect the exisiting function. Please let me know a copy past formulae so I can make the change I am not a coding expert.

Thanks,

Deepak

entry.title: Re: ABAP Proxy generation support with SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Dear @prasanthi_chavala , my user ID in S/4 is having SAP_ALL, hence should not hit any authorization error.

And for S/4 to access Cloud integration API should be via OAuth2 if SM59 already configured to use OAuth2, hence no need to assign AuthGroup_IntegrationDeveloper to our S-ID/email.

@radhika_javvaji , do correct me if I'm wrong. Btw, I am still facing issue after logoff and login. will raise an incident later.

entry.title: Secure Login Service Initial Authentication - Move away from AD Kerberos to Azure Entra
entry.content:
We implemented Secure login server several years ago and used Kerberos as initial authentication with virtual user on Netweaver Secure login server system and there after a short lived X.509 certificate is issued by Secure login service which enabled SNC SSO authentication for all SAPGUI systems. Kerberos is AD protocol and we plan to move away from on-prem AD to Azure Entra. Entra doesn't support AD, so the initial authentication piece will need to be moved from AD Kerberos SPNEGO to something else. Does SAML fit in here with same virutal user scenario ? If yes what are the high level steps i need to take care - SAML configuration to Azure with metadata exchange, perhaps new saml2 policy on SLS and what else. Please let me know your thoughts

Regards

Kal

entry.title: How to Auto Assign Check Number in Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear Experts

I am facing an issue, i am on public cloud, i have created check lot using fiori app Manage Checkbook  and I have tried everything to create payment through check, but system is not auto assigning check number.

Your help will be appreciated.

BR

Muhammad Yousuf Ali

SAP FICO Consultant

entry.title: Re: Part 5 - SAP MDG – A Stepping Stone for SAP S/4HANA Journey
entry.content: Super comprehensive with a practical use case, good work Prasanna

entry.title: Re: SAP TM Lock Trace Analysis - Unlocking Performance Insights with Internal Support Tool
entry.content: Thank you for this Blog, I did not know that there were this kind of possibilities in TM!

entry.title: SAP Public Cloud 2602 / MDG - How to Mass Import Data into existing Business Partners
entry.content:
Hello Experts,
i have a few questions about the ability to add new fieldvalues for existing business partners in mass. 
From my research so far the simplified process should work as follows:

1. Export the resprective Business Partners, with the relevant tables and fields (App: F6551A)

2. Add an additional entry (new row) with the needed values into the downloaded Exel-File 

3. Depending on the change, insert an appropiate ACTION CODE in the Action Code column. 

4. Upload the file again and start the consolidation process (App: F6553A and App: F6556A)

My questions are the following:

1. Is this functionality even in scope for the basic/free MDG Version in SAP Public Cloud 2602 ?

2. If i want to add a value for a specific table and field, should i export the complete business partner or just this table/field ? Or is there an other best practice for what tables/fields to use for the export/import ?
So far both ways havent been successfull for me, because i get a variety of errors, including that certain tables need to be included in the upload or that some fields are missing and need to be filled etc.

3. What Action Codes are available in the Public Cloud MDG ? So far i found out that, there is I - Insert; C- Copy and D - Deletion. 
Are there more ?

4. I have encountered multiple tables, where i get an error that the Action Code I is not allowed for this tabel. Does SAP provide information about what Action Code is allowed for what table/field or is this just try and error ?

Best regards
Lukas

entry.title: Re: Introduction into Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation
entry.content: How does this work in the context of data migration, for example AR migration during Go‑Live? How does the run know that it must also valuate the migrated items, and how does it calculate the delta?

entry.title: Re: Explanation of the Delta Posting Logic in Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation
entry.content: How does this work in the context of data migration, for example AR migration during Go‑Live? How does the run know that it must also valuate the migrated items, and how does it calculate the delta?

entry.title: Service in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
This blog shares highlights on Service in SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release.

  • Customer and product hierarchy in service order, service quotation, and in-house repair
  • Non-chargeable service orders
  • Add and transfer customer notes to service invoices
  • Adaptation of OData V4 APIs for service contracts
  • Service order integration with SAP Concur for expense handling

Watch my video to get a glimpse of the new features:

 

Customer and product hierarchy in service order, service quotation, and in-house repair

SAP introduced hierarchical pricing for service processes, enabling both customer hierarchy and product hierarchy to drive pricing and discounts across service quotation, service order, in-house repair, and service contracts. You can model multi-level hierarchies, assign customers and products to nodes, and maintain pricing conditions per level; standard condition types DCH1 (customer) and DPH1 (product) can be added to the sales area. Activation is done per transaction (BPC Expert persona), with hierarchy maintenance in the BP Master Specialist (customer) and Product Master Specialist (product) roles. Once set, the system applies cumulative conditions (e.g., 31% customer + 40% product) and carries pricing seamlessly across the document flow—solution order/quotation → order → confirmation → billing document request → invoicing—removing the need to re-enter discounts.

This delivers accurate, context-driven pricing without manual intervention, improving response times, reducing rework, and lowering the risk of SLA breaches. Customers get transparent, consistent pricing from quote to invoice, while service and billing teams benefit from fewer errors and faster quote-to-cash cycles. The consistency across repair and service contracts strengthens margin control and billing accuracy, ultimately improving customer trust and operational efficiency.

Watch this demo to have a complete view of the feature.

 

 

Non-chargeable service orders

​Non-chargeable service work like warranty repairs, rework, and goodwill jobs is currently poorly tracked, leaving customers without visibility into the true cost and profitability of their service contracts. This hidden, non-billable workload consumes significant labor, parts, and travel time but is not captured in existing reports or planning tools.

The solution introduces a unified non-billable item category that can be applied consistently across all service item types, including services, expenses, externally procured items, stock parts, fixed‑price bundles, and time‑and‑materials bundles. When an item is marked non-billable, it carries zero net value, is excluded from billing, and does not trigger revenue recognition; associated costs post directly to P&L upon consumption or confirmation. This behavior is enforced throughout the service process for consistency and auditability. The model also supports entitlement- and policy-driven scenarios—such as annual maintenance contract coverage, goodwill, or customer relationship management—so service teams can explicitly designate eligible items as non-billable based on automated entitlement checks and integrated NCSO workflows.

Capturing all non-chargeable labor, parts, and travel ensures true contract profitability by revealing the complete margin picture at the customer and contract level. Full visibility into warranty, rework, and goodwill effort improves capacity planning and helps managers meet SLAs with realistic workload forecasts. Aggregated NCSO insights highlight repeat failures and cost of poor quality, enabling targeted product and process improvements. Clear evidence of hidden service effort strengthens renewal negotiations and reduces unnecessary discounts, while automation of entitlement checks and workflows eliminates spreadsheet work, cuts errors, and ensures consistent, compliant service execution.

Watch this demo to have a complete view of the feature. 

 

 

Add and transfer customer notes to service invoices

As a repair-service customer, users want a detailed repair summary (including billing information and instructions) printed on the invoice, so that users do not need further clarification.

This enhancement introduces dedicated Text Note types across the repair flow—In‑House Repair, Repair Quotation, Repair Order, Repair Confirmation, and the Billing Document Request (BDR)—so customer billing-related notes and instructions are captured once and automatically propagated to follow-on documents. During processing, the system determines which text to use based on a defined priority: notes from the Repair Confirmation take precedence, followed by the Repair Order, Repair Quotation, and then notes from the Manage In‑House Repairs app. The selected billing notes are transferred into the BDR and printed on the final invoice, enabling a detailed repair summary that includes billing information and customer instructions without manual copy/paste.

By ensuring the right billing notes flow end-to-end and appear on the invoice, customers get a clear, self-contained repair summary that minimizes follow-up questions, speeds dispute resolution, and can accelerate payment. Internally, teams spend less time re-keying or reconciling notes, reducing errors and ensuring consistent, audit-ready documentation across repair stages and billing. The higher priority on confirmation notes captures the latest work details, improving accuracy and customer confidence while streamlining the handoff from service execution to billing.

Watch this demo to have a complete view of the feature.

 

 

OData V4 APIs for service contracts

SAP has adopted OData V4 for service contract management (V2 remains available but won’t receive enhancements). V4 now supports contract change processes via API: renewing items on the existing contract, renewing the entire contract (creating a new one), renewing the entire contract with new items on the existing contract, and changing the sold-to party. Address handling is enhanced so business partner addresses at header and item level can be updated via both UI and API (with similar support extended to other service transactions). Eventing was expanded with Item Created, Item Changed, and Item Deleted, and these events now support custom fields, enabling richer, extensible payloads.

Standardized API support for common contract changes reduces custom coding and manual steps, accelerating implementations and daily operations. Event-driven integrations and custom field support let teams automate downstream processes and capture organization-specific data, improving data quality and responsiveness. Moving to V4 positions customers to receive ongoing innovations, lowering long-term integration costs and improving agility.

 

Service order integration with SAP Concur for expense handling

SAP S/4HANA Cloud service orders now integrate with SAP Concur for fixed-price expense items. A “Concur Relevant” indicator is automatically available for fixed-price expense items; when selected and the order is released, the item is replicated to Concur, and the system suppresses service confirmation for that item to avoid double-booking. Processing can be triggered manually or via scheduled jobs, with monitoring in Business Network Integration/Concur monitors. In Concur, technicians submit a “Service Order Item” expense report linked to the service order, then postings are imported and acknowledged back into SAP S/4HANA Finance, creating journal entries. In 2602 this is a limited, request-based enablement, with planned 2608 improvements (e.g., activation by sales area/company code).

Field technicians submit expenses on the go via Concur’s mobile app, reducing administrative burden and speeding reimbursement. Finance and service managers gain near-real-time job costing and profitability insights while ensuring compliance with travel policies and preventing duplicate cost capture. The end-to-end flow from service order to Concur to Finance tightens controls, shortens cycles, and improves visibility into service delivery costs.

Watch this demo to have a complete view of the feature.

 

 

Feel free to follow us in the SAP community and on LinkedIn to not miss any updates.

Watch the replays of our SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Early Release Series! 

From January 12-16, 2026, we hosted a series of compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 product release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you.  You can find the recordings of your past sessions on SAP Learning. As a partner, you will find the recording and presentation for Manufacturing as well as other topics of interest via the Empowering SAP partners site.

🔍 Find all our assets  

The release 2602 showcases more 130 innovation highlights across the portfolio. Check out our release document for all the details!

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Click on the enablement wheel below and check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you:

entry.title: Re: How to find a CDS view for a classic Table in ABAP? - Cheat Sheet
entry.content: Link is expired again 😞

entry.title: CEI Invitation - Shape the Future of Financial Correspondence
entry.content:
What is CEI?

SAP's Customer Engagement Initiatives (CEI) platform, found at https://influence.sap.com, empowers SAP customers and partners by offering early access to SAP product developments. As a participant, you have the opportunity to interact directly with SAP product experts to influence future product direction.

 

Description of Planned Project

Our team manages the "Create Correspondence" and "Display Correspondence History" applications in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. We are now exploring how to leverage AI to further automate the financial correspondence workflows and increase operational efficiency.

 

Project Goals and Activities

  • Gather requirements and use cases
  • Identify existing pain points
  • Validate concepts
  • Prioritize potential features / processes

🛠️ SAP Product in Focus: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
🗓️ Estimated Effort: 1 - 3 hours call with each customer
🗣️ Language: English
📍 Format: Calls, workshops, conferences and usability testing
💡 Expected Outcomes: user-centric, harmonized solution across financial correspondences processes 

 

We’re looking for customers to join us in validating use cases, exchanging ideas, and shaping the potential future of financial correspondence.

 

👉 Register Now 👈

Registration End on Mar 15, 2026

 

 #SAP #S4HANAPublicEdition  #CustomerEngagementInitiative #Finance

entry.title: Re: SAP IBP - Profit Center Description Not Appearing in...
entry.content:
Assuming that the attribute (Profit Center Description) is assigned to the planning levels, you can try the following:

In Master Data Workbook pick one example - delete the Profit Center Description -> save -> repopulate with the same value -> save.

If it is displayed correctly afterwards,  try running "Adapt Planning Objects" application job.

entry.title: India Withholding Tax (TDS/TCS) Status Report - India Price Category Issue
entry.content:
When this app is assigned to users, they are able to view the report only; no transactional or update actions are available.

Additionally, since the report is display only, why it is classified as Advanced? If Write Access is set to No Access, the report does not open at all, even though users only require view access.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: India Withholding Tax (TDS/TCS) Status Report - India...
entry.content:
Hi all,

 

The 'India Withholding Tax (TDS/TCS) Status Report' application enable to view all the withholding tax line items for both supplier (TDS) and customer (TCS) documents for a specific reporting period.

The 'India Withholding Tax (TDS/TCS) Status Report' application access this report using the SAP_FIN_BC_GL_REPORTING_IN_PC. The catalog is not specific to the 'India Withholding Tax (TDS/TCS) Status Report' application. The catalog is a part of the multiple application and reports that are part of India localization (Ex: DRC statutory reports for India). 

Additionally, while assigning authorization roles to users, please ensure that both Read and Write access are granted. Since the process requires passing values to parameters and executing the program, write authorization is mandatory.

 

Please refer the below help document for more information,

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/634261119fec4d58970471f2c4a9a740/d276b4eb7c424ab4916ea3f0da966940.html?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION&version=LATEST

 

Best regards,

Atanu

SAP Support - S/4HANA Cloud

entry.title: Missing Employees in Executive Review | SAP SF Compensation - Executive Review | Non-EC integrated
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

In SAP SuccessFactors Compensation we are experiencing the issue that the second manager/division manager in Executive Review cannot see the employees of his direct reports. The division manager/second manager has access to the template in Executive Review, but unfortunately no employees of his direct reports are displayed.

It is a non‑EC‑integrated template. However, the division manager/second manager is already fully maintained in SAP SuccessFactors. His direct reports, as well as the employees of the direct reports, currently only have a mini‑master record in SAP SuccessFactors.

Do you have any idea what might be causing the issue that the division manager/second manager cannot see the employees of his direct reports?

Thank you in advance for your help.

Best regards,
Johannes

entry.title: Business Configuration Transport from Dev to Prod Tenant in SAP Subscription Billing
entry.content:
Dear SAP Subscription Community,

Is there any possibility for Business Configuration Transport from DEV tenant to Test / Prod tenants in SAP Subscription Billing, or do we have to do all configurations in each environment (example Markets, Products, Rate Plans, Pricing Schemas, and so on) manually?

I saw that on cTMS Subscription Billing was not from the supported types and it looks like there is nothing in this regards. 

I checked also the Roadmap of SB and the only thing I could find was the "Tenant Data Management" but not related to the transport of objects from one tenant to another more then resetting a Tenant.

Any help, hint, idea is very welcomed.
Thank you,
Doris

entry.title: Re: Welcome to the Data and Analytics Learning Group

entry.title: Re: SAPbobsCOM company connect works in visual studio but fails while publish in IIS
entry.content:
Hi @ Diego,
I face same problem like you and same environment  in Visual studio (MVC) work fine but when public to IIS not work .

My solutions : 
 I have removed  C:\windows\temp\SM_OBS_DLL  folder and restart IIS it is resolved in my side.

entry.content: Hi, try the following from https://me.sap.com/notes/1574568/E (1574568 - SAP Gateway Foundation and SAP Gateway 2.0 - Known Constraints)

entry.title: Re: Enable Microsoft Copilot and Teams to Pass Requests to Joule
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We have configured all the steps from all the 3 blogs. However, it's not working.  Getting a message" Something went wrong".  Everything seems to be correct with respect to configuration. Please help.

entry.title: Re: SAP Signavio Dictionaries
entry.content:
Hello, 

If I understand your issue correctly, I see two options :

  • Define the Responsibility for a task at the task's attribute (with a "Responsibility" custom attribute)
  • Or use of the "Additional participant" BPMN element, linked to the task.

Either of those would indeed have to be linked to a new Dictionary category called "Responsibilities" (type : Organization), which would be separated from the existing Business Roles dictionary category.

This ensures that the BPMN pool defines the Business Role, but additional responsibilities can be referenced for specific tasks.

Also in the Responsibilities category's items description, you can then put contact info as needed.

Best,

entry.title: need to change company code base on some condition in f110 at proposal stage
entry.content: need to change company code base on some condition in f110 and this need do in proposal stage , when we do it in Substitution rule it will apply when we doing the pasting not at the proposal stage

entry.title: Re: OData V4 Delta Token (@odata.deltaLink) not returned for CDS View with ODP
entry.content: Hello Dani, you can try the solution from the note1574568 - SAP Gateway Foundation and SAP Gateway 2.0 - Known Constraints: Delta Token Simple Transformation does not support Delta Token Functionality. Hence the entities which make use of Delta Token must make sure that Simple Transformation is Deactivated. To Deactivate follow the below steps - a. Goto Transaction /IWBEP/ST_ADMIN b. Check for column "Service" and "Entity Type Name" for your service/entity name using DeltaToken c. Click on corresponding value(Activated) for the entry in column "Usage" d. Popup will be shown to deactivate transformation e. Press 'Yes' to deactivate Transformation

entry.title: Re: Deletion of CI_COBL
entry.content: Thanks for the great experience feedback! It's worth a blog post for finding it more easily.

entry.title: Re: How to mass update Material Classification in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Enda,

Did you end up testing the MARA_KSSK mass upload? I tried with I (Include) in action field but system is not picking it up.

I am able to export the table, but once I try to Import there is no option to select:

And if I do not select, i get the following:
Define a valid scope to continue.

entry.title: Re: Can RSH (Resource Scheduling for asset management) be...
entry.content:
Hi,
no, unfortunately, this is not possible. RSH only integrates with the maintenance orders as demand objects to be scheduled. In some views, we also include maintenance plans and their scheduled call-offs, but not as an element to be changed/maintained from the RSH apps.
PS Networks are not included as a demand object. Should you use PS Networks as a grouping element for maintenance orders, then the WBS Element or Project can be used to filter the orders, but that's all we offer.
Best regards,
Markus

entry.title: Beyond the Workhorse: What I’ve Learned Modernizing U.S. Utilities
entry.content:
Introduction

In my decade-plus of architecting for major U.S. utilities—from the high-pressure power corridors of the Northeast to the expanding grids in the South—I’ve seen a shift in what "success" looks like. For a long time, the goal was simple: Keep the lights on. But as we hit 2026, that benchmark is failing. Aggressive decarbonization and the wave of decentralized energy (DERs) like rooftop solar and EV charging are putting a massive strain on our trusted workhorse, SAP IS-U.

1.     The Reality Check: When the "Standard" Hits the Ceiling

IS-U is a dependable system for meter-to-cash, but it was designed for a world of predictable, one-way power flow. In my recent field work, I've kept hitting three "Legacy Burdens":

  • The Batch Job Bottleneck: Waiting for overnight runs to understand grid load is a non-starter in a smart-grid world.
  • The "Spaghetti" Problem: Years of custom ABAP "Z-code" have made some environments so brittle that a simple integration takes months of testing.
  • The 2027 Clock: S/4HANA migration isn't just an upgrade; it’s a race to preserve business logic buried in 20 years of customizations.

 

2.     My Architecture Philosophy: The "Clean Core" Mindset

The biggest shift I’ve driven in recent programs is moving away from the instinct to "customize inside the ERP." By adopting a Clean Core strategy, we treat SAP BTP as our innovative layer.

This keeps the S/4HANA core stable. While the core handles the heavy transactions, the "brain" (SAP BTP) manages the fast-changing logic. This gives us speed without risking the stability of the billing engine.

3.     Practical Field Notes: Real Impact with BTP

This isn't theoretical. I’ve seen this move the needle in North American programs, including work with one of the largest utility companies in north America:

  • Integration Suite: This is the "glue" connecting AMI, weather data, and regulatory feeds without brittle, point-to-point interfaces.
  • Hindsight to Foresight: We are now using SAP Datasphere to identify transformer stress before it leads to failure.
  • The Human Element: Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) rises when we use BTP to send proactive usage alerts mid-month.

4.     A Deep Dive: The Automated Reconnection Case

I’m particularly proud of rebuilding the customer reconnection process. Traditionally, this was a multi-day headache. We turned it into a real-time event.

How we built it:

  1. Payment Trigger: Customer pays via an app (SAP Build).
  2. Instant Clearing: SAP Integration Suite pushes the update to FI-CA.
  3. Decision & Execution: We applied a 3-layered governance protocol to ensure only "Safe-to-Reconnect" signals hit the AMI smart meters.

4.     Lessons from the Trenches: What Leaders Need to Know

This isn't just about clicking buttons in the BTP cockpit; it's a cultural shift. If you are leading a team, remember:

  • Close the Skill Gap: Veteran ABAP teams have the domain knowledge—help them translate that into Node.js and CAP.
  • Avoid "App Sprawl": Without governance, you’ll end up with a hundred disconnected micro-apps.
  • Data Residency: For U.S. utilities, compliance for billing data must be built into the architecture from Day 1.

Conclusion: The Future is Composed

Across the programs I’ve led, the winning pattern is clear: S/4HANA is the Heart, BTP is the Brain, and IoT/Integration is the Senses.

SAP IS-U has served us well, but the energy transition demands more agility. The question isn't if we should move to BTP—it's how quickly we can use it to stop being reactive and start being proactive.

I'd love to hear from other architects: What's the messiest "Z-code" logic you're currently trying to move out of your core? Let's discuss in the comments.

entry.title: Re: Cannot deploy CAP application in SAP BTP Trial – stuck at "Deploying the Application"
entry.content: Hi @MioYasutake, Thanks for the quick response — sorry for the confusion. I realized this is not a deployment issue yet. I was following “Prepare for Production”: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/prep-for-prod.html After running: cds add hana --for production `mvn cds:watch` fails (I executed it from the `srv` folder; prompt shows `user: srv $`). Key error: [ERROR] The 'src' folder '/home/user/projects/incident-management/srv/srv' for build task 'java' does not exist Log excerpt: [INFO] Executing [npx, -c, cds build --for java] in .../incident-management/srv [ERROR] The 'src' folder '.../incident-management/srv/srv' for build task 'java' does not exist Could you advise what configuration might cause cds build to look for a double “srv” path (`.../srv/srv`) after `cds add hana --for production`? If needed, I can share my `.cdsrc.json` and relevant parts of `srv/pom.xml`. (If you prefer, I can also try running `mvn -pl srv cds:watch` from the project root and share the result.) Thanks!

entry.title: Re: SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025
entry.content:
@wagener-mark - Can you please advise : - 

1 - What is meant by importing the CSR from STRUST 

2 - What exactly needs to be done in Destinations on BTP

3 - What is meant by 'We needed to add the ECS proxy to the service (don't forget the allow-list SR).'

4 - How did you Create a separate PSE in STRUST ? We have added all the certificates in SSL client SSL Client (Standard) and when testing the connection in appldestcc , we get the message 'Error while receiving client'

@BinWang - How do we connect with SAP Joule Readiness Service ? 

Rahul

entry.title: Re: Any standard utility in ABAP to retrieve a version of abap dev object from Remote system
entry.content: I understand that you're interested by the last version only, and you want to copy from PROD to DEV. I recommend using a transport of copies, but you must copy the whole object (R3TR). For instance, if you find out that only one method of a class is different, you'll have to transport the whole class. Your DEV system must be configured with VERS_AT_IMP = ALWAYS to keep the version history. In case your object source system is not DEV (the system ID of your DEV system), I recommend changing it to DEV.

entry.title: Financial Management and Revenue Recognition for Project-based Sales
entry.content:
Introduction

Project‑based sales require tight financial integration: costs from logistics and project activities, revenues from billing plans, and margins must align in real time. Modern S/4HANA Cloud capabilities enable exactly this by linking sales orders and projects into one financial flow, ensuring immediate cost and revenue attribution and consistent margin visibility across the lifecycle.

This blog collection provides executive insights into how event‑based revenue recognition, unified posting logic, and streamlined period‑end processes strengthen governance and accelerate decision‑making for project‑based sales.

Blog Collection

 
👉 Explore the series for practical guidance, architectural patterns, and deeper insights
 
Financial Accounting for project-based sales in S/4HANA Cloud
What’s New in Finance for Engineer Products and Systems (based on Engineer-to-order)
Discrete Industries in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition 2302

entry.title: Re: OData V4 Delta Token (@odata.deltaLink) not returned...
entry.content:
Hello, 

I encountered the same issue with an OData V2 service generated via transaction SEGW.

The solution described in SAP Note 1574568 (SAP Gateway Foundation and SAP Gateway 2.0 – Known Constraints) resolved the DeltaLink issue in the DeltaInit response forOData V2. It may also be applicable to OData V4.

  1.  Delta Token

Simple Transformation does not support Delta Token Functionality. Hence the entities which make use of Delta Token must make sure that Simple Transformation is Deactivated.

To Deactivate follow the below steps -

             a.  Goto Transaction /IWBEP/ST_ADMIN
             b.  Check for column "Service" and "Entity Type Name" for your service/entity name using DeltaToken
             c.  Click on corresponding value(Activated) for the entry in column "Usage"
             d.  Popup will be shown to deactivate transformation
             e.  Press 'Yes' to deactivate Transformation

entry.title: Re: JMS Mastery Series Part 1 - The Async Awakening
entry.content:
@RubikWal 

Great question!

For data replication, use async SOAP API unless you have a specific reason not to.

Possible reasons to use OData API instead:

- user-triggered integration (not real A2A data replication) requiring immediate validation feedback

- processing is consistently fast and predictable for expected batch sizes and volumes

Bear in mind also that sometimes you can find ODATA behaves in async way. Pls check sales order ODATA API which can work both SYNC and ASYNC, also with minimal response SYNC.

Important consideration: End-to-end async flow (with async SOAP API on receiver side) is more demanding from design and development perspective. If the backend cannot handle errors internally, it requires creating separate iFlows for either callback/status query API (CI polls backend for results) or error notification handling (backend pushes errors back to CI). This additional complexity should be weighed against the benefits of full decoupling.



br

Mateusz

entry.title: Re: IFRS 18 and SAP S/4HANA Group Reporting: What It Means and How to Get Ahead

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA 2025: gCTS 'Synchronous Push' issue with GitL...
entry.content:
Hi,

After reporting the issue to SAP, they have released an SAP Note to fix it.
3714602 - gCTS ABAP: Fix authentication error for Gitlab repositories 

The note has also been included in:
3378978 - gCTS: Collection of Corrections to Known Issues 

Regards,
Alfonso.

entry.title: Re: Is it supported to map a master‑data custom field int...
entry.content: Yes your three steps are correct (define → populate → map). Use the BAdI for logic, not direct DMEE modification

entry.title: After matrix manager change via Manager transfer - new manager does not receive any notification
entry.content: Currently when matrix manager changes the manager transfer does happen but there is no notification that goes to new matrix manager saying that -"Now you are the new matrix manager and have the form in your inbox for your review". I want to understand is there any feasibility to create such notification when form moves to new matrix manager?

entry.title: Re: How I can consume a project stock in Maintenance Orde...
entry.content:
Dear @Egbert_de_Jong,

Point 1. Is Project Stock (special stock Q) possible in Public Cloud for work orders / maintenance?

Yes but only if the WBS belongs to a project profile that supports project stock (valuated stock). SAP delivered (predefined) enterprise project profiles are set up like this Investment Project = Valuated Stock, Project with Revenue = Valuated Stock, while Overhead / Statistical = No Stock

SAP Notes below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3204437

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3557238

So:

  • If your WBS is in Investment Project or Project with Revenue you can hold and report Project Stock Q it will show in stock overviews as project stock

  • If your WBS is in Overhead Project or Statistical Project project stock is not enabled and postings/requests for project stock will be blocked

Point 2. Consuming project stock to a Maintenance Order is supported

For the withdrawal itself Movement type 261 GI for an order, including maintenance orders supports special stock indicator Q, so the technical capability exists the blocker is whether your WBS is allowed to have project stock from point 1

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3697643

Point 3. Why migration allowed project stock only for some projects/WBS

This is almost certainly because those project types map to different project profiles / inventory valuation:

  • The allowed ones correspond to Investment Project / Project with Revenue (valuated stock)

  • The blocked ones correspond to Overhead/Statistical (no stock)

SAP explicitly documents the restriction Account Assignment Category Q with a WBS outside Investment Project / Project with Revenue isn’t supported inventory valuation mismatch / no stock valuation

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3204437

Point 4. Workarounds

Option A: If you need inventory tracked as Project Stock stock + value by WBS

  • Use a project type/profile that supports it Investment Project or Project with Revenue

  • If you want to avoid AuC side effects, SAP even suggests a workaround to use an Investment Project with an investment profile like 000002 so no AuCs are generated

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3557238

Option B: If your projects must remain Overhead/Statistical but no project stock

Then you cannot keep inventory as Project Stock Q. Typical workaround is:

  • keep materials in plant unrestricted stock

  • issue them to the maintenance order/WBS so costs hit the WBS but inventory is not segregated as project stock

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft Excel and SAP Datasphere connection

entry.title: Business Partner Master Data in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced digital economy, the integrity, security, and accessibility of master data are paramount to operational excellence. High-quality Business Partner data forms the foundation of countless critical processes, from procurement and sales to finance and compliance. I am pleased to share some of the latest innovations within SAP S/4HANA Cloud that are designed to elevate your business partner master data management strategy.

SAP Cloud ERP is part of SAP Business Suite that brings AI, data, and applications together into a fully integrated system. This community is open for anyone in the cloud ERP ecosystem–customers, partners, and SAP experts.  

To strengthen security and data integrity, we implement header-level authorization checks and proactive duplicate detection to prevent unauthorized access and ensure high-quality, reliable data from the outset. At the same time, onward replication across multisystem landscapes and conversational AI through Joule enable seamless synchronization and intuitive access to information, driving efficiency, transparency, and faster decision-making across the enterprise.


This post will provide an overview of four significant new features: 
1. Enhanced security through header-level authorization checks.
2. Proactive data quality management with an API-based duplicate check.
3. Streamlined data synchronization via onward replication in multisystem landscapes.
4. Efficient data access using our conversational AI, Joule.

1. Enable Business Partner Header-Level Authorization Checks Based on Data Controller and Purpose

Data privacy and compliance are non-negotiable. To provide more granular control over sensitive information, we have introduced the ability to enable authorization checks at the business partner header level based on the combination of a Data Controller and a Purpose.

Feature Scope:
This feature allows for the assignment of specific data controller and purpose combinations directly to a business partner record. Subsequently, the system can enforce authorization checks based on these assignments.

Key Benefits:
The primary advantage is a significant enhancement in access control. With this feature activated, a user must be explicitly authorized for the specific data controller and purpose assigned to a business partner to gain access to that record. This ensures that only relevant personnel can view or process sensitive data, strengthening your data governance and compliance posture in line with regulations like GDPR.

 

Business partner: assignment of purposes along with data controllers

2. Business Partner A2X OData API Operation for Duplicate Check

Maintaining a clean and duplicate-free master data repository is a constant challenge, especially in integrated environments. To address this proactively, we have extended the capabilities of our core Business Partner OData service.

Feature Scope:
A new operation for performing a duplicate check has been added to the `API_BUSINESS_PARTNER` A2X OData service. This operation can be invoked for Business Partners, Customers, and Suppliers.

Key Benefits:
This new functionality empowers external applications to identify potential duplicates before a new master data record is created via the API. By embedding this check into the creation process, organizations can prevent the introduction of redundant data at the source. This is a crucial step toward retaining high master data quality, reducing downstream cleanup efforts, and ensuring process consistency across the enterprise.

Please refer to following Blogs:

  • Using Business Partner OData API (Saranga)
  • Deep Payload for Business Partner OData API (Mani)

Duplicate Check in Business Partner A2X

 

3. Business Partner Integration: Onward Replication in a Multisystem Landscape

For organizations managing complex system landscapes, ensuring data consistency across all systems is a major undertaking. Our new onward replication capability simplifies this process significantly.

Feature Scope:
This feature allows you to trigger the onward replication of incoming business partner data from your SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP system to all other connected systems in your landscape. Activation is managed through a simple self-service configuration user interface (SSCUI).

Key Benefits:
This innovation enables your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system to function as a true master data hub. When a business partner is created or changed, the system automatically distributes these updates to all subscribed downstream systems. This automated synchronization ensures data consistency across the entire landscape, reduces manual integration efforts, and minimizes the risk of data-related process failures.

Onward Replication of Business Partner Master Data in SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP

 

Community Blog: Onward Replication of Business Partner master data in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

4. Joule as Efficient Access to Business Partner, Customer, and Supplier Master Data

In our pursuit of a more intelligent and user-friendly ERP experience, we have integrated our conversational AI, Joule, with master data management.

Feature Scope:
Joule now allows users to efficiently access attributes of Business Partner, Customer, and Supplier master data objects using natural language queries.

Key Benefits:
This feature fundamentally streamlines the process of data retrieval for business users. Instead of navigating through multiple Fiori apps and screens, a user can simply ask Joule a question, such as "Show me the addresses for Business Partner 100345" or "What are the dunning data details for Customer XYZ?". The key benefits include:

- Enhanced Productivity: Immediate access to necessary data saves time and effort, allowing analysts to focus on decision-making rather than data retrieval.
- Reduced UI Clicks: Joule provides extremely quick information about attributes without requiring users to open dedicated applications.
- Intuitive Interaction: Natural language input makes data access more accessible to a wider range of users.
BP, Customer, Supplier Master Data in SAP Joule

Joule can provide details ranging from a business partner overview, roles, and relationships to specific customer sales area data and supplier purchasing organization details.

 


Community Blog; Exploring Business Partner Master Data with SAP Joule, the AI Co-pilot

These four features represent a significant step forward in making master data management more secure, intelligent, and efficient within SAP S/4HANA Cloud. By enhancing security, improving data quality at the point of entry, simplifying landscape-wide synchronization, and accelerating user access, we are empowering our customers to build a more robust and reliable data foundation for their enterprise.

📽️ Watch the replays of our SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Early Release Series! 

From January 12-16, we hosted a series of 22 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find the recording and presentation for LoB/Industry/Topic as well as 21 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime. 

🔎 Find all our assets 

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Re: SAP Service and Asset Manager Phase model Work Orders
entry.content: SSAM does not allow the user to Create Phased based Work order types from the client(Mobile app). Phase orders should not be created by mobile users. They should follow the phase processes. Orders should not be created directly to document work. Rather Either a Minor or Emergency work request should be raised. This allows for some of the phases to be skipped. SSAM supports both Minor and Emergency work requests.

entry.title: Re: New POD 2.0 Framework: The New Generation of SAP Digital Manufacturing Worker UIs
entry.content: next one please 🙂

entry.title: Clarification Needed on SAP User Locking and Deactivation Policy in S/4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
I would like to request clarification on the SAP user blocking/locking and deactivation process followed in current setup.

Could you please provide guidance on the following points:

  1. Automatic Blocking Due to Inactivity
    After how long of no login activity is a user automatically blocked/locked in SAP?
  2. Deactivation After Blocking
    Is there a subsequent deactivation step after a user is blocked?
    • If yes, does this follow IT policy?
    • Is any confirmation or approval required before deactivation?
  3. User Notifications
    Are users notified in advance before their account is blocked or deactivated?

This information will help us provide accurate support to users experiencing access issues.

entry.title: Italy SDI B2G invoice send failing – cert status error 0010 (S4/CPI)
entry.content:
Hi team,

We have an issue. When we send invoices to Italy SDI (Italy Send Invoice) for B2G through S4/CPI, we receive the following error message "Invalid Certificate Status" error code 0010. However, all certificates are valid according to CPI Monitoring. Receiving invoices is still working.

Can anyone please help with this? How can we fix this issue?

entry.title: Re: Changing Stock Status from Quality Inspection to Unre...
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA, stock in Quality Inspection can only be released to Unrestricted through Quality Management processes. That’s why your attempt with MIGO (movement type 321) failed — the system enforces that inspection stock must be changed via QM, not directly through Inventory Management.

Options to resolve

Check inspection lot creation settings
Verify in material master (QM view) whether inspection type is active. If no inspection lot was created, the stock may have been posted incorrectly into inspection stock.

If inspection lot exists
Use QA32 (or Manage Usage Decisions app in Fiori) to record the usage decision. This will automatically move stock from Quality Inspection to Unrestricted (movement type 321).

If inspection lot does not exist
You cannot use QA32. So options are:

  • Post a reversal of the original goods movement (e.g., cancel GR) and repost into unrestricted.
  • Or, if business process allows, activate inspection type and create a manual inspection lot, then make usage decision.

entry.title: Re: Handling Job Info deletions using Compound Employee-Delta & Period Delta in SuccessFactors
entry.content: Thanks for this very detailed and insightful post on a not too common subject. Look forward to more such posts

entry.title: Solution Manager SP level update (upgrade) impact on ChaRM
entry.content:
Impact on Charm During the Solution Manager SP level update (upgrade)


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Solution Manager SP level update (upgrade) impact on...
entry.content:
Please read KBA 3715315 - The Impact of SolMan SP Level Updates on ChaRM Operations

In the first instance, the configuration for Change Request Management is not affected by the SP level update, provided mandatory SOLMAN_SETUP activities are completed post-update. However, issues may arise if you have modified standard transaction types before the update without copying the modifications to customer namespace.

entry.title: Re: yaai & yaai_ui – Open-Source AI Tools for ABAP Developers
entry.content: @ChristianSilva Is that any way to get the return parameter in the Internal table instead of string so we can able to see the output in table format

entry.title: Re: Partial payment for oversea supplier invoice and reco...
entry.content:
Good day @Prasit_Arpornrat 

Thank you for your question.

Yes, your understanding is correct. In standard AP clearing, realised FX gain or loss is posted at full clearing of the invoice item. For partial payment, there is no standard setting in public cloud to force full realised FX posting for that partial settlement portion in the same way as final clearing.

So, for your scenario, there is no standard AP process in public cloud, realised FX is tied to clearing completion. There is no standard workaround that fully replicates “realized per partial payment” without changing process design.

See the following references for more details:
https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3687934

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/abefd353ca9f4408e10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/b8c08e0197454541a11f8d46ef1ab96e/ec2aa818846a43d2852598fe62b32c72.html 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: will pMRP consider open sales order and Production or...
entry.content: @raghvan yes I don't see it is considering existing loads or reservations, only stock it can consider but other planned receipts are not considered.

entry.title: Search help (F4) for the Person Responsible field is not working in IW21
entry.content: Hello everyone, The search help (F4) for the Person Responsible field is not working while creating a notification, even though the relevant HR master data is properly maintained. Could anyone know what might be causing this issue ?

entry.title: Re: SAP PO to SAP CI Migration - Simplifying File-Based Scenarios with SMB Adapter in SAP CI
entry.content:
@Hello @morteninge ,

Thank your for the question.

It’s true that legacy SMB versions (especially SMB1) are insecure and should not be used. It’s important to use a modern version like SMB 3.1.1 along with encryption and signing which is already mentioned in this blog.

Furthermore, The connectivity should remain strictly within internal network boundaries, From an integration perspective, access should be routed via SAP Cloud Connector(or EIC). With SAP Cloud Connector in place, Connectivity to SMB flows internally through that secure tunnel. The SMB server remains inside the network and is not publicly exposed.

Hope this clarifies.

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Help!! MDF object not working in standard/custom compensation statement
entry.content: I would need more details. I recently was able to get the MDF table to work on a standard statement. You need to ensure the field in the MDF table are configured properly and the fields in the statement are as well.

entry.title: Re: Capability to Replicate Composite Custom MDF objects...
entry.content: Yes, this is possible.

entry.title: Re: Missing Employees in Executive Review | SAP SF Compensation - Executive Review | Non-EC integrat
entry.content: This sounds like it may be an RBP issue but it is difficult to confirm without seeing the system. Make sure you have an RBP role for that user and that the target population is set up to include those employees. Also, remember Executive Review only pulls data from the template so if you have these employees who are ineligible, they will not appear on forms and thus executive review. So I would first check if the users are on forms, then check RBP. Also remember that filters in executive review may have been changed and they are designed to limit visibility to specific populations. There could be other reasons but these are the most common and a good place to start.

entry.title: Full and final settlement India payroll
entry.content:
Hi Team,

In India, the newly implemented 2025 Labour Codes require that the full and final settlement (FnF) be completed within two working days following an employee's resignation, dismissal, or termination.

Is there any option in SAP payroll to process directly in the system? Any suggestion will be helpful !!

Thanks

entry.title: Re: How can i restrict access to custom CDS View Data by...
entry.content:
As far as i understand, this must work via Restriction Type/Fields. 
Nevertheless, this is still not working for me, thou i think i do everything correctly.

I WAS ABLE to restrict data using IAM App (and it behave exactly as i expect!) but not using Restriction Types. 

Though have a feeling, that this case MUST BE covered by Restriction Types because they were designed especially for restrict data access.

So, put the whole case e2e here, to follow my logic chain. If i miss something (or do incorrectly) please point on this.

1 Create Data Element

2 Create Authorization Field with Data Element

3 Create Authorization Object 

4 Create Restriction Field and assign Authorization Field

5 Create Restriction Type and Assign Authorization Object and Restriction Field

6 Create Business Catalog and assign Restriction Type

7 Create Business Role and assign Business Catalog + user

8 Maintain Restrictions. As values i put Supplier numbers here

9 Create custom CDS View Entity based on I_Supplier.

10 Create Access Control by pfcg_auth, filter data by Supllier

11 Execute data preview. Here i expect to see 2 lines with Supplier = 0055300017, 0033300019. But nothing.

entry.title: Re: How can i restrict access to custom CDS View Data by...
entry.content:
Hi Filipp.

Thanks for reply. 

That was exactly what i did. But this approach still does not work for me. 

I can restrict data using IAM app, but not with Restriction Types.

Please review my reply above where i put all steps i performed, maybe you can point on mistake. 

Regards

Vitali

entry.title: SAP HANA Cloud: Frequently asked questions, learn SAP HANA Cloud and take your certification
entry.content:
Why should I use a cloud database?

The top reasons for embracing cloud databases are faster access to data, consumption-based pricing, elastic scale, and a fully managed solution to reduce maintenance.

How does a cloud database help me accelerate to the cloud?

You can extend on-premise database landscapes to the cloud with expanded capacity for computing and storage to begin your cloud journey.

How does SAP HANA Cloud relate to SAP Business Technology Platform?

SAP HANA Cloud is the database management foundation of SAP Business Technology Platform. This platform encompasses database and data management, analytics, application development, and integration, as well as intelligent technologies.

 The SAP HANA Cloud adapters are different from the open connectors in SAP Business Technology Platform, however. SAP HANA Cloud provides an SAP HANA Cloud smart data integration (SDI) adapter that integrates with the open connectors framework in SAP Business Technology Platform.

How does SAP HANA Cloud relate to SAP Business Data Cloud?

Built for high performance and reliability, SAP HANA Cloud is the trusted database foundation that powers strategic solutions like SAP Business Data Cloud, ensuring your data drives innovation and insight.  SAP HANA Cloud can consume the curated data products from Business Data Cloud.

Is SAP HANA Cloud a general-purpose database?

SAP HANA Cloud is a single database that can serve many analytical and transactional use cases, including OLTP and OLAP. It adds flexibility for multimodel features, developing applications, and more.

What is the difference between SAP HANA and SAP HANA Cloud?

The SAP HANA Cloud database offers the same power and performance that you might already be familiar with in the SAP HANA database. However, SAP HANA Cloud is built natively for the cloud, which means greater connectivity and several new features. It’s also a database as a service, offering greater flexibility.

 With SAP HANA Cloud, you don’t have to worry about managing hardware, operating systems, backups, and other types of maintenance. All of this is taken care of by SAP.

Does SAP HANA Cloud only work with SAP data?

SAP HANA Cloud can access SAP and third-party data management practices, including federation and replication methods. We use smart data access (SDA) and smart data integration (SDI) and access includes ODBC, REST, data provisioning agent (DPAgent), and JDBC.

Learn more about SAP HANA Cloud with our SAP Learning:

Introducing and Preparing SAP HANA Cloud

All Courses and Learning Journeys

Live Sessions

Get and stay certified

Reimagining certification in the age of AI

SAP Certification practical exams (Step-by-step guide)

System-based assessment FAQs

Scenario-based assessment FAQs

Good learn, journey, discovery and have a good experience using our SAP Learning 

 I see you on next post.

entry.title: Re: SAP CC JDK 21 Upgrade
entry.content:

Thanks,

Vishnu.

entry.title: Bangalore Stammtisch - Jan 29
entry.content:
I was happy to be part of the 1st 2026 SAP Event at Bangalore  and it was this SAP Stammtish at the Mindset consulting office.  I reached there by 5.00 PM and the venue was already set and people started walking in. For many if you are not aware of SAP Stammtisch, it is informal gathering , Short event where people Share, Learn and Network especially with SAP background.

we were greeted by the dyanmic team of Mindset which includes @Parvathi shankar ( ParUu and @Kamalika. The team helped to settle down with initial quick introduction.

The event started with a quick Quiz in mentimeter hosted by @Vara Prasad Thatikonda ( From Daimler). This was intresting and the fastest fingers with correct answers will be the winner. It was kind of a icebreaker but there were some hard questions also .

 Post the quiz we started our discussion on the  using VS code for ABAP , then to AI application, SAP Joule, SAP BDC, Integration suite. These were great dsicussion. There were discussion on the real use case how the customer was  using the BTP services especially the SAP Translation Hub and how they saved lot of efforts. These were great insights from @vara prasad. We also heard from @Abhishek pal on the usage of the Joule in the SAP sucess factor .

The discussion really opened up some venues and it was a food for the thoughts. The time flew fast and it was already 8 Pm and many wanted to head back to home challengin the Bangalore traffic.

we ended with our ritual, that is the group photo !! We also plan to meet at the SAP Inside track 2026 at Bangalore.

Once again thank for Mindset consulting team for hosting us and being a great supporter of the SAP community.

entry.title: Re: SAP Asset Manager 2110 E BM 305 cannot processed in plugin mode HTTPS
entry.content: Hi can you please let me know how you resolved this error in SSAM? we are facing same issue in SSAM2410

entry.title: Re: Tomcat 9.0 EOL and BI4.3
entry.content: Thanks for your reply! The EOL for Tomcat 9.0 is now officially 3/31/2027 (https://lists.apache.org/thread/jq5xnvb7t3qg0zkh8k8gmvh7gsybfjv4). I understand BI4.3 goes out of mainstream support this year, but it is still in CSM support through 2027. What happens if a security vulnerability is found in Tomcat 9.0 in 2027?

entry.title: S4HC 2602 output management: maintain email address for real estate invoices (21Q)
entry.content:
Dear community,

We would like to send out real estate lease-out invoices to the lessee in S4HC 2602.

  • We have set up the output parameter determination according to test script 21Q. Furthermore, we also tried various fields on BP master data (general, customer, customer fin acc).
  • Also, in the OPD, a default email in the tab Receiving did not help.

Unfortunately, in the Manage Real Estate Output Requests app , the email recipients section of an invoice remains empty and the system throws a message the address cannot be initial.

Which field(s) should be maintained in order to send out mail communication to lessees?

Thank you in advance for your insights!

Cedric

entry.title: Re: (ABAP 7.50) SAP GUI showing label area as a blank box...
entry.content: Make sure you are running  144.0.3719.93 or later,

entry.title: Re: Questions on flexible workflow in purchase order
entry.content:
Thank you Lakshmi. I do have a couple of clarifications though

For your response to your first response

"you need to configure the agent determination so that the workflow checks the creator’s role/limit before assigning approval. Practically, this means: Define responsibility rules by amount thresholds and role. Ensure the workflow condition excludes the creator if they already hold the authority for that threshold."

So are you saying we can handle these using teams and responsibilities or through config or via coding using agent determination via BADI? Can we set threshold rules in teams and responsibilities app?

For your second response

"Escalation/Alternate Approvers: Configure escalation paths or alternate approvers in the workflow definition so that if no agent is found, it routes to the next higher role (e.g., Manager)."

Is this via config or again using agent determination via BADI?

entry.title: Re: Masking Sensitive Fields in BP Transaction Based on Vendor Account Group
entry.content: Hi RaymondGiuseppi, This looks like a possible solution, we also have similar requirements. but this note is mostly focused on hiding a field if another field is hidden or so. Is there a way to include/inject a logic that the fields are hidden when accessed via a certain users, and is visible when accessed via another users. The soution given is for hiding this based on context, but i don't think we can have username in context.

entry.title: Re: (ABAP 7.50) SAP GUI showing label area as a blank box...
entry.content: Microsoft Edge 144.0.3719.93 or later is required for this solution.

entry.title: Re: Masking Sensitive Fields in BP Transaction Based on Vendor Account Group
entry.content: Hi RaymondGiuseppi, This looks like a possible solution, we also have similar requirements. Looking to hide address information of certain BPs (in an auth group) but only for a set of users. Other super users should be able to view this address. This note you shared is mostly focused on hiding a field if another field is hidden. Do you know any other way to include/inject a logic that the fields are hidden when accessed via a certain users, and is visible when accessed via another users. The soution given is for hiding this based on context, but i don't think we can have username in context.

entry.title: Re: Handling Job Info deletions using Compound Employee-Delta & Period Delta in SuccessFactors
entry.content: This is incredibly helpful for anyone looking to understand how Compound Employee handles deletions a topic that is both niche and often quite complex.

entry.title: Re: VSCode - Unable to deploy or preview to ABAP on-prem...
entry.content:
Hi,

If you are setting HTTP and HTTPS via your operating system environment variables, then please remove the proxy config from both the ui5 and deploy YAML configurations.

To eliminate our tooling, this step might work; as it removes any third party software as a contributing factor in your connectivity issues;

https://github.com/SAP-samples/fiori-tools-samples/blob/main/misc/sslcerts/README.md#validate-tls-connectivity-nodejs

You can ignore the error regarding the settings.json, this is being used for other flows to ensure the appropriate settings are being migrated.

Without access to the full log, you can enable debug mode to review the HTTP requests/responses which might help understand your issues, for example on windows;

set DEBUG=* && npm run deploy

or if using PowerShell

$env:DEBUG="*"; npm run deploy

I would also suggest speaking with your IT Admin and confirming if your ABAP target host needs to be added to NO_PROXY, as not all requests need to be routed through a proxy. Please note, ERR_UNEXPECTED_PROXY_AUTH typically means:

- The client did not expect a proxy authentication challenge, or
- The authentication flow is misconfigured, unsupported, or incomplete.

John

entry.title: SAP BOFC 10.1 SP29 - Scheduled Task do not start
entry.content:
Hello,

since we have migrated our SAP BOFC to a new server (With Upgrade from SP21 to 29) the scheduled task do not start anymore. 

  • It is possible to start tasks them manually. 
  • There is low load at the system. The porcesses are not occupied.
  • Neither Logs of Financial Consolidation Client, CtServer nor Windows Event Log show any warnings or errors. 

I am on with sap support since 2 month and now hope anyone can give me a deep dive of this schedules. I know, that the task schedules are stored in a database table. But which task reads ths and triggers job runs?

Best Regards Andreas

P.S.: Migration was done by

  • Installing new FC at a new server,
  • using the Promotion Management Wizard of BOE to get the BOE Content migrated, ,
  • copying the database to a new database with backup restore and
  • migrating of the data source FCAdmin-Console.

entry.title: Timeout in SolMan/ChaRM fails to change a ticket status although import into next system succeeds
entry.content:
I've got a SolMan/ChaRM ticket referring to a big modifiable workbench transport request (with released tasks), I'm passing its status to the quality system, which creates a Transport Of Copies (ToC) which contains the object list of the original transport request, and imports it into the quality system.

While the import runs, the ChaRM Web user interface is frozen and shows in progress (spinning wheel).

After 10 minutes, there's a timeout in the ChaRM Web user interface (a timeout message is shown, probably managed by the SolMan BSP application; sorry I didn't take a screenshot).

I refresh the Web page and I see that the status remains "In Development".

I can see that the ToC is imported completely a few minutes later and has a return code 0 (success).

How to "repair" the ticket status, make it go to the next status?

Complementary question: do you know which setting to configure to increase the concerned timeout? (the one for the Web page?) It's not rdisp/max_wprun_time which is 5000 seconds.

Thanks.

Sandra

entry.title: Re: Timeout in SolMan/ChaRM fails to change a ticket status although import into next system succeed
entry.content:
Answer only to the main question. I don't know for the timeout setting.

To repair the ticket status, do this:

  1. Release and import manually the workbench transport request into the quality system
  2. Open the ChaRM ticket and change to the next status (in my case, I change two statuses: one to create and import the ToC, one to release and import the workbench transport request).
    ChaRM sees that the transport request has been released and imported successfully, so it changes the ticket status immediately without complaining.

entry.title: Re: Elevating Data Privacy in SAP CAP based GenAI Applications with CAP LLM Plugin
entry.content: @AkashAmarendraIs there a published reference implementation that shows how to use the CAP LLM plugin, SAP AI Core/GenAI Hub and the HANA vector engine to integrate generative AI into an S/4HANA on-premises business process?

entry.title: Re: Building a Custom Digital Manufacturing POD Plugin — The New Easy Way
entry.content:
Hi

How can I connect to destinations configured in same BTP subaccount if I want to connect to a deployed CAP application in my custom plugin if I am deploying the plugin using this method

entry.title: Re: A SAP BTP Kyma Encryption/Decryption Microservice for ALL Contexts (e.g. SAP Datasphere/BDC/ABAP
entry.content: Thank you very much, Stefan! This is a great post, very valuable. I am convinced that we can reuse this knowledge with other customers who have large and sensitive data transfers

entry.title: Re: SAP Public Cloud 2602 / MDG - How to Mass Import Data...
entry.content:
Dear @LukasKuhn,

Question 1.

Yes these capabilities exist in Public Cloud as embedded Master Data Governance functions delivered via best-practice scope items notably:

  • 1N3 Master Data Consolidation for Business Partner
  • 1RK Mass Load and Mass Maintenance for Business Partner (In Deprecation)
  • 7MI Mass Load and Mass Maintenance for Business Partners

More details below:

https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/s4hana-cloud/master-data

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/ee9ee0ca4c3942068ea584d2f929b5b1/2d8beb7d7341406ebb9a1f33129c4d54.html?version=2508.00

Practical Public Cloud prerequisite that often matters in real systems:

  • You may need the feature toggle MDC_BP_CLOUD_READY enabled SAP explicitly references it for missing templates / setup of the new mass apps

SAP Note below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003660827

 Question 2.

Public Cloud best practice (SAP-supported example) export only the needed table(s) and use that export as your upload template. SAP Public Cloud Note example for inserting an Industry Sector does not export a full BP, it exports only table BUT0IS via Select Scope-> Select Tables then you maintain the required technical columns like _ACTION_CODE, SOURCE_ID, etc. and upload the file

SAP Note below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003506176

So the rule of thumb in Public Cloud mass processing:

  • Export the smallest set of tables that contains the data you want to add/change using Select Tables
  • Do not build a file from scratch always start from the system export because it includes the required technical columns and key fields the upload expects
  • If upload errors say table X must be included or field Y is missing that means the app requires additional dependent tables/mandatory fields for that object in that process ->re-export including the missing tables and rework using the new template

Question 3.

What SAP explicitly documents for Public Cloud BP mass apps is:

  • I = Insert
  • For updating existing entries, _ACTION_CODE is not needed leave it empty

SAP does not provide a universal public list like I/C/D/etc. for all tables, so for Public Cloud the safe statement is:

  • Guaranteed/documented I for inserts and blank for updates

Question 4.

No Public Matrix is provided that lists allowed action codes per table

In Public Cloud you generally learn this from:

  • the app validation message and/or
  • a Public Cloud Note describing the specific table/field restriction or alternative table to use

For example SAP states the Industry Sector field BUT000-IND_SECTOR is obsolete and the supported way is inserting via table BUT0IS with _ACTION_CODE = I. That’s exactly the kind of I not allowed / wrong place issue

Also, if you hit issues like template/process missing in the new mass apps, SAP points to enabling MDC_BP_CLOUD_READY

SAP Note below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003660827

IMPORTANT: SAP has also the separate product call SAP Master Data Governance, Cloud Edition

More details below:

https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/master-data-governance/faq

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: New POD 2.0 Framework: Template-Based Design Instead of POD Types
entry.content: @ThanosG I just vibe coded a simple order filter for POD2.0 in under 15 mins, so it should not be a big deal in the end. 🙂

entry.title: Problems hiding and showing table columns
entry.content:
Hello experts, I have a problem.
I need to hide table columns based on certain conditions.
I get the table object by ID and then all the columns.
Then I use oColumn.setVisible(), and everything works. However, the problem is that in the table personalization, this column is marked as selected, with a check mark.
Is it possible to affect the table settings after changing the column properties?
All tables are described on the backend and Table Type 

ResponsiveTable(sap.ui.mdc.table)

entry.title: SAP GUI for Java 8.10 available on developer trial download page
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

This is Guilherme, Team Lead for SAP GUI for Java.

For users and developers who do not have access to the SAP GUI for Java installer through their administration, or who do not have an S-user to download it from the SAP Software Center, SAP GUI for Java is also available on SAP’s trials download page:

https://developers.sap.com/trials-downloads.html

Or directly via:
https://www.sap.com/products/try-sap/trials-downloads.html?search=sap+gui+for+java

entry.title: check is not getting assigned in public cloud
entry.content:
Dear Experts

I am using fiori app Post Outgoing Payment in public cloud, how can we auto assign check in public cloud, i-e i am in starter system, though i have created check lot for house bank, i have tried Automatic Payment app, but check is not getting assigned in any case.

How can i do so?

BR

Muhammad Yousuf Ali
